arguments.task: Research Otter AI using official first-party pages only where possible. Open https://help.otter.ai and find evidence for: whether Otter AI offers a public API or developer docs; authentication methods mentioned for integrations or API access; pricing or access tier requirements; support or changelog pages; and any links to GitHub or MCP/server pages if they exist. Prefer links on otter.ai or help.otter.ai. Return concise extracted findings with exact source URLs. If first-party pages do not provide enough evidence, you may use web search within the browser to look for official Otter AI developer/API docs and official pricing/access pages, plus GitHub pages for an MCP if any exists.

input.thought: Use browser automation to inspect official Otter AI help and related first-party pages for API, auth, access, and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: AI meeting assistant for recording, transcribing, summarizing, and querying conversations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official help docs show two distinct surfaces: the Otter MCP Server connects via OAuth from supported clients, while the Otter.ai Public API uses API keys sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header; docs also mention a limit of 2 API keys.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official Public API help article states the Otter.ai Public API is available for the Enterprise Plan. This means self-serve free access is not sufficient for API toolkit development, though an MCP surface appears publicly documented.

api_surface.breadth: Documented Public API endpoints include at least users, conversations, utterances, and meeting media retrieval; official help docs describe Enterprise-only REST API usage with Bearer authentication. Separately, Otter documents a hosted MCP endpoint at https://mcp.otter.ai/mcp for clients such as Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and Perplexity.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official help article documents an Otter MCP Server with hosted endpoint https://mcp.otter.ai/mcp and OAuth connection flow. Evidence gathered does not verify an official public GitHub repository for the MCP server.

raw_result.blocker: First-party evidence indicates the Public API is Enterprise-only, so building a broad direct API toolkit requires paid/enterprise access; however, an official hosted MCP server exists and may enable narrower agent integrations via OAuth.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public rate-limit details were verified from the gathered evidence. The help docs do note a limit of 2 API keys for the Public API, but request quotas/throttling remain unverified.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official help center contains a dedicated Public API article and a separate MCP Server article, indicating active support for agent-facing access patterns. The help center also has a Product updates section, suggesting some ongoing product/change communication.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain uncertain from the gathered evidence alone: the exact pricing/access mechanics on the pricing page were not extracted, no official public GitHub/MCP registry listing was verified, and no explicit rate-limit policy was confirmed. Verdict depends on whether downstream toolkit can rely on MCP instead of Enterprise REST API.

result.description: AI meeting assistant for recording, transcribing, summarizing, and querying conversations.
result.auth_notes: Official help docs show two distinct surfaces: the Otter MCP Server connects via OAuth from supported clients, while the Otter.ai Public API uses API keys sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header; docs also mention a limit of 2 API keys.
result.gating_reason: The official Public API help article states the Otter.ai Public API is available for the Enterprise Plan. This means self-serve free access is not sufficient for API toolkit development, though an MCP surface appears publicly documented.

api_surface.breadth: Documented Public API endpoints include at least users, conversations, utterances, and meeting media retrieval; official help docs describe Enterprise-only REST API usage with Bearer authentication. Separately, Otter documents a hosted MCP endpoint at https://mcp.otter.ai/mcp for clients such as Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and Perplexity.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official help article documents an Otter MCP Server with hosted endpoint https://mcp.otter.ai/mcp and OAuth connection flow. Evidence gathered does not verify an official public GitHub repository for the MCP server.

result.blocker: First-party evidence indicates the Public API is Enterprise-only, so building a broad direct API toolkit requires paid/enterprise access; however, an official hosted MCP server exists and may enable narrower agent integrations via OAuth.
result.rate_limit_notes: No public rate-limit details were verified from the gathered evidence. The help docs do note a limit of 2 API keys for the Public API, but request quotas/throttling remain unverified.
result.notable_signals: Official help center contains a dedicated Public API article and a separate MCP Server article, indicating active support for agent-facing access patterns. The help center also has a Product updates section, suggesting some ongoing product/change communication.
result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain uncertain from the gathered evidence alone: the exact pricing/access mechanics on the pricing page were not extracted, no official public GitHub/MCP registry listing was verified, and no explicit rate-limit policy was confirmed. Verdict depends on whether downstream toolkit can rely on MCP instead of Enterprise REST API.
